title: The Hidden State as Memory
concept: The hidden state is updated at each step based on the current input and the previous hidden state, accumulating historical context.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of the 'hidden state' in sequential AI?

options.text: A running memory that updates over time

exercises.explain: The hidden state serves as the memory of a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), updating step-by-step.

cards.title: AI's Running Memory
cards.body: How do computers remember what happened a few seconds ago in a video or a text? Instead of starting fresh at every single word, AI uses a clever trick called a 'hidden state' to carry memories forward in time!

cards.title: What is the Hidden State?
cards.body: The hidden state is a vector of numbers that acts as the model's memory. At every step in a sequence, the network takes the new input and mixes it with the previous hidden state to calculate a brand new, updated hidden state. This allows the network to remember past context.

cards.title: The Reading Detective
cards.body: Imagine you are reading a long mystery novel. As you read each word, you don't forget the previous pages. Instead, you keep a running summary of the plot in your head. When you read a new clue (current input), you combine it with your mental summary (previous hidden state) to update your understanding of the story (new hidden state).

cards.title: Processing a Sentence
cards.body: If an RNN reads the sentence "The sky is blue", at step 1 it processes "The" and creates a starting memory. At step 2, it combines the word "sky" with the memory of "The". By the time it reaches "blue", its hidden state contains a blended memory of the entire sentence context.

cards.title: The Snowball Effect
cards.body: Many people think AI models completely rewrite their memory from scratch at every step, or only remember the single last word. In reality, the hidden state acts like a snowball, gathering and carrying forward bits of information from everything it has seen so far.

cards.title: Memory vs. No Memory
cards.body: See how memory changes as new information comes in, compared to a system with no memory tracking.

left.text: Combines the word 'blue' with the memory of 'sky is' to understand the context.

right.text: Sees the word 'blue' in total isolation, with no idea what came before it.

exercises.prompt: What two things are combined to create the updated hidden state at any given step?

options.text: Only the very first word in the sequence
The current input and the previous hidden state

exercises.explain: The hidden state depends on both the fresh incoming data (current input) and the accumulated memory of the past (previous hidden state).

exercises.prompt: True or False: The hidden state allows an RNN to remember information from the beginning of a sentence even when it is processing the end.
exercises.explain: Because the hidden state updates step-by-step, it naturally carries information from earlier steps forward to the end of the sequence.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to describe the RNN's memory mechanism.
exercises.sentence: An RNN carries historical context forward by updating its [blank] at each step.
exercises.explain: The hidden state is the active memory vector that gets updated at each step of the sequence.

exercises.prompt: Which AI model correctly explains how it maintains memory using a hidden state?

options.text: AI Model A: 'I update my hidden state at each step by mixing the new input with my previous state, keeping a running summary.'
AI Model B: 'I throw away my memory at every step and only look at the current word to stay fast.'
AI Model C: 'I store each word in a completely separate file and never let them mix together.'

exercises.explain: AI Model A correctly describes how the hidden state evolves step-by-step to build up context. Model B incorrectly claims it ignores the past, and Model C incorrectly claims it stores everything in separate, unlinked files.

exercises.prompt: When a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) processes a sequence, how does it update its hidden state at each step?

options.text: It resets its memory to zero at every new word
It combines the new input with the previous hidden state
It only looks at the very first word of the sentence
It skips steps to process the entire sequence at once

exercises.explain: An RNN is designed to process sequences step-by-step. At every single step, it must look at the incoming item and mix it with its memory (the previous hidden state) to update its understanding of the whole sequence.
